guideline_references: Modified Duke Criteria for Infective Endocarditis
Therapeutic Guidelines Australia: Antibiotic — Infective endocarditis
ESC 2023 Guidelines for the management of endocarditis
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is an after-hours physically-present clinician who auscultated a new early diastolic (aortic regurgitation) murmur and palpated splenomegaly and took vitals. The AI Doctor CANNOT auscultate, palpate or measure vitals without a device, so it is scored on RECOGNISING the do-not-miss constellation (prolonged fever + painful fingertip nodules + splinter haemorrhages + known bicuspid aortic valve + toxic appearance) and ESCALATING to emergency hospital assessment, NOT on making the murmur/echo/culture diagnosis.
The murmur, splenomegaly, blood cultures and transoesophageal echo are sealed clinician/hospital findings — they must never leak into the AI-readable patient fields.
Age inferred as ~52 adult (source gives weight 70 kg and a 3 kg loss but no age) — flagged.
Sex inferred as male (not stated in source; bicuspid aortic valve is somewhat more common in males) — flagged.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD), source case CDV-015.
